title: Corporate Risk & Solvency Framework
description: A comprehensive controls framework evaluating systemic organizational risks across liquidity, debt structures, and operational margins.

metrics.benchmark: Ideal value < 1.2. Ratios exceeding 2.0 signal excessive insolvency risk.
metrics.mitigation: Refinance expensive high-interest short-term notes into long-term fixed bonds, and redirect excess operating cash flows to de-leverage.

metrics.benchmark: Ideal value >= 1.5. Ratios below 1.0 indicate severe near-term working capital shortfalls.
metrics.mitigation: Enforce tight accounts receivable timelines (target collections under 30 days) and set up revolving backup credit lines.

metrics.benchmark: Ideal value >= 10%. Margins under 2% indicate extreme vulnerability to pricing shocks.
metrics.mitigation: Conduct a strict zero-based budgeting overhaul and transition toward high-margin recurring license services.

metrics.benchmark: Ideal value >= 5% growth. Negative values signal rapid operational cash bleed.
metrics.mitigation: Halt non-essential capital expenditures and optimize working capital allocations.
